attacking_style: Pursues long-range breakaways (500km+ cumulative distance) rather than explosive late attacks. Targets stages where breakaway can survive, defending inside final 3km when necessary.
team_tactics: Operates with autonomy in breakaways; no evidence of dedicated team setup or domestique support. Appears to be given freedom for opportunistic moves.
terrain_preference: Stages where breakaways have mathematical survival chances. Excels in scenarios requiring multi-day recovery and repeated long-distance efforts rather than explosive climbing.
vulnerability: Lacks finishing power in reduced sprints; relies on breakaway survival rather than ability to drop companions or win contested finishes. Vulnerable when peloton commits to chase.
key_patterns: Exceptional multi-day breakaway capacity: 500-700km+ cumulative distance across single Grand Tour (2 observations)
Targets secondary classifications systematically, hunting intermediate sprint points from breakaways (2 observations)
Opportunistic rather than stage-focused: prioritizes classification points over stage wins (2 observations)
Requires breakaway survival for success; won by meters when peloton nearly caught group (1 win)
form_trajectory: positive: demonstrated exceptional recovery capacity and strategic discipline throughout 2019 Giro with sustained breakaway success
